title: Docker-format image in Manifest List `application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json`
body:
### Discussed in https://github.com/containers/buildah/discussions/4549
Originally posted by **andrewm-aero** February 1, 2023 I'm not sure if this is a bug, and if so, on whose side, but the [latest dockerhub ubuntu images](https://hub.docker.com/layers/library/ubuntu/focal-20230126/images/sha256-d5d4814ffb155d588f10ec8926d9e1cd09b6d41a3110d2f42959e2fc37f6d0b4?context=explore) (pushed today) are not pull-able by buildah, complaining that they have an `unsupported OCIv1 media type: "application/vnd.docker.container.image.v1+json"`. Docker, on the other hand, pulls it just fine. Taking it apart, it does in fact appear to be an OCIv1 wrapper with an entire docker v1 image as an element, rather than separate layers and configs as you'd expect. I've never heard of such a thing. Is this valid, or does this suggest an error on Ubuntu's part? [This code](https://github.com/containers/image/blob/abae97a8306d61cc66addba6b3d0e1ad81d83045/manifest/oci.go#L44) appears to be what determines this (non-)compatibility, and sure enough, that media type is not listed.

comments.body: We're facing the same issue too with both buildah and podman.
I think issue might be with how ubuntu list was created but docker supports it so I think podman should support it too.

Looks like a bug in c/image to me since the manifest includes
```
  "mediaType": "application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json",
  "schemaVersion": 2
}
```

Same applies to the layer's media type.  But I am honestly not sure what to do. This will break in all versions of c/image and I think for valid reasons. The fact that Docker eats it is unfortunate though :(
@rcj4747 are you the right contact person for the Ubuntu images?

> @rcj4747 are you the right contact person for the Ubuntu images?

Not, not for a little while now, but @j5awry or @philroche might be about to get you a step closer to the right owners.
They seem to be aware of the issue: https://github.com/docker-library/official-images/pull/13950

:+1: 

I'll be posting updates here -> https://github.com/docker-library/official-images/issues/13983

In short, some tools are quite forgiving of this mixing of mediaTypes, while others aren't. We are building new images with all media types being oci-specific.

Thanks a lot, @cjdcordeiro!

I am going to close the issue here and ask folks to follow/engage in https://github.com/docker-library/official-images/issues/13983 instead.
quick update: PR with the fix- > https://github.com/docker-library/official-images/pull/13988
